data.title: Bull run on D-Street: Sensex soars 1,000 pts, Nifty reclaims 25k as metal, auto, IT stocks rally
data.description:
Market at noon: Sensex and Nifty remain flat but in red while treading soft global cues; broader markets outperform in green

After trading flat until noon, Indian benchmarks Sensex and Nifty saw swift rise on May 15 by rising over 1% each amid easing geopolitical tensions. At 1:39 pm, the Sensex was up 1,000 points or 1.23 percent at 82,330.35, and the Nifty was up 321.95 points or 1.31 percent at 24,988.85. About 2,302 shares advanced, 1,135 shares declined, and 128 shares were unchanged. Small cap and mid cap remained flat although in green, with marginal gains.

Metal, IT and Auto sectors were leading the rally by trading at a gain of 1-1.6%.

Top gainers at 12:30 pm were Tata Motors, Adani Ports, Hero Motocorp, JSW Steel and HCL Tech. Top losers were PowerGrid, Indusind Bank, NTPC, Kotak Mahindra and Infosys.

Across mid caps, the top few gainers were Linde India, Zee Entertain, Yes Bank, Star Health and  Cochin Shipyard. The top few losers across the midcap space were Muthoot Finance, Godrej Industries, Persistent Systems, Torrent Power and Dixon Technologies.

Across small caps, the top few gainers were Tilaknagar Industries, Triveni Turbine and Nelcast. While the top few losers were Hitachi Energy, CESC and Mannapuram Finance.

The last trading session's closing revealed that 82% of stocks are hovering more than 20% below all-time peaks. Thus, reflecting at a narrow market rebound. Strikingly, 67% (1689) of actively traded stocks are trading at levels more than 30% from their peaks, and 22% (547 stocks) at more than 50%. These all-time highs were hit at different points since June 2024, but the gap from current levels highlights how uneven the market recovery has been.

While the Nifty 50 has been trading above 200-DMA for a few days, this is the first time since January that the index crossed and stayed consistently above the mark. Technical analysts continue to remain bullish in near-term outlook.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Persistent Systems shares fall 3% as major US client's CEO leaves abruptly, firm withdraws forecast
data.description:
Persistent Systems shares fall 3% as major US client's CEO leaves abruptly, firm withdraws forecast

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Persistent Systems fell sharply on Thursday after a major US client, UnitedHealth Group, saw its Chief Executive Officer step down abruptly.  and pulled back its 2025 financial guidance.

Persistent’s stock dropped 3.59 percent in intraday trade on the NSE to hit a low of Rs 5,552 per share. The scrip later trimmed some losses but was still down 2.93 percent at Rs 5,589.5 at around 12:30 PM. The stock has declined nearly 5 percent over the past three trading sessions.

The latest slide was triggered by developments at UnitedHealth Group, whose shares tumbled over 15 percent on Tuesday after CEO Andrew Witty resigned suddenly citing personal reasons. The US-based healthcare giant also withdrew its financial forecast for next year.

UnitedHealth is among Persistent’s top clients and falls in the $100 million-plus revenue bracket, brokerage Dolat Capital said in a client note, reported CNBC-TV18.

Nearly 60 percent of Persistent’s revenue in FY25 came from the healthcare segment, the brokerage added, cautioning that growth may now shift towards non-healthcare verticals in the near term.

UnitedHealth has faced a series of issues over the last year. These include a large-scale cyberattack on its tech arm impacting around 190 million people, a federal probe into its Medicare billing practices and a rise in medical expenses that caught analysts off guard.

In a separate development, The Wall Street Journal reported that the company is currently under investigation for alleged Medicare fraud.

UnitedHealth also made headlines last year after Brian Thompson, the CEO of its insurance division, was killed in New York just ahead of a key investor meet. The company later posted its first earnings miss since the 2008 financial crisis in April this year.

Dolat Capital warned that Persistent Systems’ premium valuations may come under pressure. Persistent Systems is a Pune-headquartered IT services firm.

(With Inputs from Reuters) 

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
